name: Apple, Raisin, and Rosemary Dressing
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Dry Madeira or 1 tablespoon Brandy
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 225°F.
2. Spread the bread crumbs on a baking sheet and dry toast them in the oven for 5 to 10 minutes.
3. Melt the butter in a large skillet over the water.
4. Add the celery and onions, and sauté until the onions have absorbed the butter and are limp. Do not brown them.
5. Add the apples, raisins, bread crumbs, baking powder, rosemary, and Madeira or brandy to the skillet. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Toss well with a fork. If the stuffing seems overly dry, add an egg or a little chicken stock.
7. Stuff a crown roast of pork or a turkey according to your pork/turkey baking times/temperatures, or place in a casserole dish and bake uncovered at 325°F until browned, about 25 minutes.
